plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce sector and tech concentration.
Reduce SPY to lower single-name concentration and free up capital.
Add to QQQI to increase income exposure and lower overall risk.
Initiate a new position in LEU, a strong scanner setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns. Expect slower growth compared to other modes.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to lower overall leverage.
Reduce SMH and QQQ to decrease sector and index concentration.
Trim SPY to bring it within the single-name concentration limit.
Initiate new positions in AVGO and AAPL, strong scanner setups.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
Balance broad market exposure with quality tech holdings.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets about 2.5% monthly return by reducing riskier assets and concentration while adding quality names. It aims for growth with controlled drawdowns. Leverage is kept at a moderate level.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL for amplified gains.
Add to SMH and CRWD to concentrate in high-conviction sectors.
Trim SPY and sell QQQI to reduce broad index and income exposure.
Initiate a new position in GUSH, a top-tier scanner setup.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer to maximize invested capital.
Accept higher potential drawdowns for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for approximately 4% monthly return by leaning into leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risk of rapid losses due to leverage and daily rebalancing of ETFs. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all high-risk leveraged ETFs and volatile tech/semiconductor holdings.
Significantly increase allocation to QQQI for income generation.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and free up capital for income assets.
Initiate a position in XOM for stable income and value exposure.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for stability.
Prioritize consistent monthly distributions over capital appreciation.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through steady distributions. It drastically reduces risk by selling growth-oriented and leveraged assets, focusing on income-generating ETFs and stable names. NAV can still fall in market downturns, but the goal is reliable cash flow.
